evidence: Packages map clearly to domain capabilities: api/, config/, exploration/, model/, query/, utils/, viz/, indexer/ — a new engineer can read the tree and explain what each subtree owns
model/package-info.java explicitly documents which types are DTOs vs model objects with derived accessors, and states that no I/O or HTTP concerns belong there
Handler classes in api/ are each focused on a subdomain: SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — each has a single, named HTTP concern
GrammarConfigSerializer lives in api/ (correct: it is an API-layer presenter for config data) and GrammarConfig/RelationConfig live in config/ (correct: pure data) — ownership is coherent

CLAUDE.md code-structure section still lists ~15 files that no longer exist: query/SemanticFieldExplorer.java, query/QueryResults.java, query/Token.java, query/TokenWindow.java, query/LemmaPredicate.java, query/TagPredicate.java, and others. SemanticFieldExplorer moved to exploration/, QueryResults moved to model/. A new engineer reading this doc would look in the wrong directories.

ExploreResponseAssemblerTest lives in src/test/java/.../model/ but imports and tests ExploreResponseAssembler from api/ — the test is in the wrong package subtree, obscuring where presentation tests should live

strengths: Package decomposition is genuinely domain-aligned: api/, config/, exploration/, model/, query/, utils/, viz/ each have a clear, single-sentence mandate
model/package-info.java explicitly governs what belongs in the model package, preventing boundary drift
Handler class split in api/ (Sketch/Exploration/Concordance/Visualization/CorpusQuery) means each HTTP subdomain has a named owner — change locality is good
GrammarConfig/GrammarConfigLoader separation correctly isolates file-IO from the data object callers depend on
RelationConfig record cleanly separates data from pattern-building logic (RelationPatternBuilder), demonstrating consistent decomposition style across the config subsystem
high_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are documentation drift rather than structural problems: the codebase has evolved correctly but the CLAUDE.md structure map was not updated, and one test class is filed in the wrong subpackage.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The live code is well-decomposed and ownership is predictable — this is solidly above a 'significant drag' (60) baseline. The main deduction is that project contracts (CLAUDE.md structure section) have drifted from reality: a new engineer using that doc would look in the wrong directories for core classes. That plus the misplaced test bring it to 76 rather than 82+, where it would sit if docs were trustworthy.

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md code-structure map lists ~15 files that no longer exist, misdirecting new engineers
evidence: CLAUDE.md lists query/SemanticFieldExplorer.java — class is now in exploration/
CLAUDE.md lists query/QueryResults.java — class is now in model/

CLAUDE.md lists query/Token.java, query/TokenWindow.java, query/CQLVerifier.java, query/LemmaPredicate.java, query/TagPredicate.java, query/PosGroupPredicate.java and others — none of these files exist
CLAUDE.md lists api/PatternSubstitution.java — file does not exist in api/
issues.suggestion: Update the CLAUDE.md '## Code Structure' section to reflect the current file tree. Run 'find src/main -name '*.java' | sort' and regenerate the tree, removing entries for deleted files and adding entries for newly introduced files such as exploration/CollocateProfileComparator.java, exploration/MultiSeedExplorer.java, config/RelationPatternBuilder.java, config/RelationUtils.java, model/ExplorationOptions.java, model/SingleSeedExplorationOptions.java, model/FetchExamplesOptions.java.

issues.summary: ExploreResponseAssemblerTest lives in model/ test package but tests an api/ class
evidence: ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java is under .../word_sketch/model/ in the test tree
Its import is 'import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.api.ExploreResponseAssembler' — the class being tested is in the api package
All other api/ tests (HandlersTest, ExplorationHandlersTest, VisualizationHandlersTest, etc.) live under .../word_sketch/api/ in the test tree
issues.suggestion: Move ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java to src/test/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/api/ to co-locate it with the class under test and match the convention used by every other api/ test.

root_causes: Documentation drift: code structure evolved (SemanticFieldExplorer moved to exploration/, QueryResults moved to model/) but CLAUDE.md was not updated
